topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The moment the transcript landed on my desk, a strange, light fluttering sensation started in my chest and spread outward. I stared at the red mark, then at the number, then back at the paper, a slow, wide smile stretching across my face until my cheeks ached. "No way," I whispered, the sound barely audible over the hum of the fluorescent lights. My hands began to tremble slightly, and I couldn't stop tapping my pen against the wood, a rapid, almost frantic rhythm. It felt like a sudden burst of pure, unadulterated joy, a feeling so big it made my stomach flip. I practically bounced in my chair, unable to contain the sudden surge of elation.

[story 2]

When Professor Albright handed back the final scores, I didn't even bother looking at the actual percentage. I just snatched the paper, my fingers brushing against the disappointing numeral, and let out a sharp, involuntary laugh that quickly morphed into a wide, unrestrained grin. A warmth flooded my limbs, a feeling of buoyant lightness that made my shoulders relax completely. I leaned back in my seat, letting out a deep, satisfied sigh, a sound of pure contentment escaping my lips. I started humming a little tune, my eyes sparkling as I reread the mark, a feeling of triumph bubbling up inside me, intoxicating and wonderful.

[story 3]

My breath hitched. I picked up the graded essay, the stark 'D-' glaring back at me. Instead of sinking into despair, a strange energy surged through me. I threw my head back and let out a loud, unrestrained whoop of laughter that echoed slightly in the quiet library corner. A giddy lightness took over my entire frame; I started pacing the small space between the bookshelves, my steps quick and bouncy. I looked around, catching the eye of a nearby student, and offered a dazzling, almost manic smile, a feeling of sheer, unbridled elation making my whole body vibrate with excitement.
